title: Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate small single-record flows (covered by 22.38.1); targets bulk operations ≥1GB or ≥10k records.
evidence: Saved search 'bulk_export_cross_border' (every 10 min) outputs bulk export operations whose destination geography lacks adequacy or SCC cover.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.44 general principle for transfers is directly enforced by blocking bulk exports without safeguards.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.44 (International transfers — general principle) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.5: Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.45 adequacy decision list membership is directly queried.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.45 (Transfers via adequacy decision) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.5: Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.28 (ICT third-party risk) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.5: Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction.

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK GDPR Art.45 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.5: Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction.

derivationSource.divergenceNote: UK adequacy decisions managed by ICO/UK government, not EU Commission.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A SQL dump of 250k customer records is written to an S3 bucket in a non-adequate jurisdiction; the UC fires within 10 min.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A bulk export to an adequacy-listed or SCC-covered jurisdiction does NOT fire.

dataSources: DB export audit logs, cloud storage PUT events, DLP bulk-operation classifications, sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv.
spl:
index=db_audit action=export OR (index=cloud_storage action=PutObject bytes>1073741824) earliest=-10m
| lookup data_classification.csv object_key OUTPUT classification regulated_scope
| where isnotnull(regulated_scope)
| `get_geolocation(dest_ip)`
| lookup sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv country as dest_country OUTPUT scc_active adequacy_decision
| where isnull(scc_active) AND isnull(adequacy_decision)
| table _time user object_key classification regulated_scope dest_country bytes
description: Intersects bulk export operations (DB dumps, large cloud uploads) with destination geolocation and the sanctioned-transfer catalogue. Bulk exports targeting non-adequate jurisdictions without SCCs are a category of breach that requires immediate executive escalation.
value: Turns post-hoc 'where did our customer dataset go?' incident forensics into real-time prevention.
implementation: (1) Ensure DB and cloud storage layers emit size + destination; (2) maintain classification and jurisdiction lookups; (3) schedule every 10 min; (4) SOAR auto-blocks destination if exception policy permits.
visualization: World-map of bulk exports, table of findings with destination geography, single-value 'bulk exports within sanctioned geographies'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service

knownFalsePositives: Bona-fide DR or migration operations authorised under a dedicated exception ID must be logged to a migrations.csv allow-list.
grandmaExplanation: Turns post-hoc 'where did our customer dataset go?' incident forensics into real-time prevention — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: DB export audit logs, cloud storage PUT events, DLP bulk-operation classifications, sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure DB and cloud storage layers emit size + destination; (2) maintain classification and jurisdiction lookups; (3) schedule every 10 min; (4) SOAR auto-blocks destination if exception policy permits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=db_audit action=export OR (index=cloud_storage action=PutObject bytes>1073741824) earliest=-10m
| lookup data_classification.csv object_key OUTPUT classification regulated_scope
| where isnotnull(regulated_scope)
| `get_geolocation(dest_ip)`
| lookup sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv country as dest_country OUTPUT scc_active adequacy_decision
| where isnull(scc_active) AND isnull(adequacy_decision)
| table _time user object_key classification regulated_scope dest_country bytes
```

Understanding this SPL

**Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction** — Turns post-hoc 'where did our customer dataset go?' incident forensics into real-time prevention.

Documented **Data sources**: DB export audit logs, cloud storage PUT events, DLP bulk-operation classifications, sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: db_audit, cloud_storage.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=db_audit, index=cloud_storage, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(regulated_scope)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Invokes macro `get_geolocation(dest_ip)` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(scc_active) AND isnull(adequacy_decision)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction**): table _time user object_key classification regulated_scope dest_country bytes


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: World-map of bulk exports, table of findings with destination geography, single-value 'bulk exports within sanctioned geographies'.
